title: Social Worker

rationales.taskAutomation: Intake screening, case notes, and benefits-matching are increasingly automatable, but home visits, crisis intervention, and the therapeutic relationship are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: Case-management and benefits-eligibility AI tools are maturing quickly inside major EHR and human-services platforms, though far from universal.
rationales.adoption: Public and nonprofit social-services agencies are chronically underfunded and slow to roll out new software relative to the private sector.
rationales.agenticExposure: Intake triage and eligibility-matching are back-office slices an agent can plausibly own, but the core relationship-based casework cannot be delegated.

tasksAtRisk: Intake form completion and benefits-eligibility screening
tasksGrowing: Crisis intervention and in-person safety assessment
Building trust with clients over time
Navigating complex family and legal situations
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 21-1029, p25-p75 rounded
insight: The paperwork half of social work is getting real automation help, which is good news if it frees caseworkers for the in-person visits and trust-building that no tool can substitute for. Agencies that adopt these tools fastest are still the exception, not the rule, in the public sector.
